footer_order: Cancel (left, t-outline) → Confirm (right, t-primary or t-danger)

confirmation.use: Destructive actions — delete, remove, revoke. Required before any destructive operation.
confirmation.body_pattern: State the consequence explicitly — what will be permanently removed.
confirmation.confirm_variant: t-danger (red) — NEVER purple for destructive confirm
confirmation.example_html:
Delete "CVE-2024-1234"?
This will permanently remove the finding and all associated notes.

form.use: Create or edit flows that don't warrant a full page.
form.example_html: Add Integration

side-panel.description: Drawer that slides in from the right. Used for detail views without leaving context.
side-panel.open_state: Add .open class to both overlay and panel
side-panel.example_html: Asset Details

do: Always use a modal for destructive actions — name the item and state the consequence.
Cancel is always left, Confirm is always right in modal footer.
Use t-danger (red) for destructive confirms — never t-primary (purple).
Close button (×) always in modal header top-right.
Trap focus inside modal while open.
dont: Never auto-close a destructive confirmation modal.
Never put two primary buttons in a modal footer.
Never skip the confirmation modal for delete/remove/revoke actions.
Never use t-primary for a destructive confirm — always t-danger.
Never open a modal inside another modal.

react.note: Use Radix Dialog for modals — handles focus trap, escape key, and accessibility automatically.
